defaults.og_image_alt: Travel Vaccine Advisor — required and recommended travel vaccines by country

home.title: Travel Vaccine Advisor — Find Out Which Vaccines You Need
home.description: Search any country and see which travel vaccines CDC recommends, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic. Plain-English.

alerts.title: Current Travel Health Alerts — Travel Vaccine Advisor
alerts.description: Current CDC travel health notices, outbreaks, and regional alerts for destinations tracked on Travel Vaccine Advisor. Updated as CDC updates.

about.title: About Travel Vaccine Advisor
about.description: Travel Vaccine Advisor is a free information site. All recommendations trace to CDC Travelers' Health. We are not a clinic and do not sell vaccines.

disclaimer.title: Medical Disclaimer — Travel Vaccine Advisor
disclaimer.description: Travel Vaccine Advisor is informational only — not medical advice. Always confirm recommendations with a licensed travel health doctor before departure.

thailand.title: Vaccines for Thailand — Required & Recommended
thailand.description: Planning a trip to Thailand? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Thailand. Depending on your itinerary, your doctor may also discuss Japanese encephalitis, rabies, or chikungunya.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 9 months and older, including airport transits over 12 hours.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Thailand should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

india.title: Vaccines for India — Required & Recommended
india.description: Planning a trip to India? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for India?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to India. Your doctor may also discuss Japanese encephalitis, rabies, chikungunya, or cholera depending on where you're going.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for India?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you're arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to India should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

mexico.title: Vaccines for Mexico — Required & Recommended
mexico.description: Planning a trip to Mexico? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Mexico?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Mexico. Your doctor may also discuss rabies or chikungunya depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Are any vaccines required for entry to Mexico?

acceptedAnswer.text: No vaccines are required for general entry to Mexico. Travel vaccine decisions should be based on CDC recommendations and your itinerary, not entry rules.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Mexico should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Indonesia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Indonesia. Depending on which islands you visit, your doctor may also discuss Japanese encephalitis, rabies, or chikungunya.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Indonesia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Indonesia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

vietnam.description: Planning a trip to Vietnam? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Vietnam?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Vietnam. Japanese encephalitis and rabies are worth discussing if you're heading to rural areas or staying long term.

mainEntity.name: Are any vaccines required for entry to Vietnam?

acceptedAnswer.text: No vaccines are required for general entry to Vietnam. Travel vaccine decisions should be based on CDC recommendations and your itinerary, not entry rules.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Vietnam should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

japan.title: Vaccines for Japan — Required & Recommended
japan.description: Planning a trip to Japan? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Japan has the lowest infectious-disease risk of our destinations. CDC lists Hepatitis B as the main travel vaccine. Hepatitis A, Japanese encephalitis, and rabies only come up for specific plans.

mainEntity.name: Are any vaccines required for entry to Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: No vaccines are required for general entry to Japan. Travel vaccine decisions should be based on CDC recommendations and your itinerary, not entry rules.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Japan should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

kenya.title: Vaccines for Kenya — Required & Recommended
kenya.description: Planning a trip to Kenya? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Kenya?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, typhoid, and yellow fever for most travellers to Kenya. Your doctor may also discuss rabies, cholera, meningococcal disease, and mpox depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Kenya?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is recommended for most travellers to Kenya and is required for travellers arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk, for those aged 1 year and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Kenya should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever must be given at least 10 days before travel, so earlier is better.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Egypt?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Egypt. Rabies may come up if you'll be around street dogs or doing a long stay.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Egypt?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Egypt should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

peru.title: Vaccines for Peru — Required & Recommended
peru.description: Planning a trip to Peru? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Peru?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Peru. Yellow fever, rabies, and chikungunya may also come up if you're heading to the Amazon lowlands.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Peru?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever is not required for entry to Peru, but CDC recommends it for travellers visiting Amazon regions below about 2,500 metres, including Iquitos and Puerto Maldonado.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Peru should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever must be given at least 10 days before travel, so earlier is better.

brazil.description: Planning a trip to Brazil? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Brazil?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, typhoid, and yellow fever for most travellers to Brazil. Your doctor may also discuss rabies and chikungunya depending on whether you're heading to the Amazon or staying on the coast.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Brazil?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is recommended by CDC for most travellers to Brazil and is required for entry from a country with yellow fever transmission risk for travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Brazil should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever must be given at least 10 days before travel, so earlier is better.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Morocco should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B for most travellers to the UAE. Typhoid and rabies depend on your itinerary. Travellers continuing to Saudi Arabia for Hajj or Umrah should also discuss meningococcal.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for the UAE?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to the UAE should I get vaccines?

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Tanzania?

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Tanzania should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever must be given at least 10 days before travel, so earlier is better.

laos.title: Vaccines for Laos — Required & Recommended
laos.description: Planning a trip to Laos? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Laos?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Laos. Your doctor may also discuss Japanese encephalitis, rabies, or chikungunya depending on your route.

mainEntity.name: Do I need malaria pills for Laos?

acceptedAnswer.text: Malaria risk is mainly in southern provinces such as Champasak and Savannakhet. Luang Prabang and Vientiane are not malaria zones, so most tourist itineraries don't need pills.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Laos should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

nepal.title: Vaccines for Nepal — Required & Recommended
nepal.description: Planning a trip to Nepal? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Nepal?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Nepal. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B, Japanese encephalitis, cholera, or rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Do I need malaria pills for trekking in Nepal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Typical Himalayan treks are not malaria zones. Risk is limited to lowland Sudurpashchim and Karnali provinces below 2,000 m.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Nepal should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to the Maldives. Rabies may come up for longer stays or specific activities.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Maldives?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Maldives should I get vaccines?

china.title: Vaccines for China — Required & Recommended
china.description: Planning a trip to China? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for China?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to China. Your doctor may also discuss Japanese encephalitis, rabies, or tick-borne encephalitis depending on your route.

mainEntity.name: Can I drink the tap water in China?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Tap water is not drinkable in China, even in major cities. Use bottled or boiled water for drinking and brushing teeth.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to China should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

south-korea.title: Vaccines for South Korea — Required & Recommended
south-korea.description: Planning a trip to South Korea? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for South Korea?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC lists typhoid for most travellers to South Korea, with Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B as decisions to make with your doctor based on itinerary. Japanese encephalitis and rabies come up for specific plans.

mainEntity.name: Do I need malaria pills for South Korea?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most itineraries do not. Risk is limited March to December in rural northern parts of Incheon, Gangwon, and Gyeonggi provinces, including the DMZ. Seoul, Busan, and Jeju are not malaria zones.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to South Korea should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

taiwan.title: Vaccines for Taiwan — Required & Recommended
taiwan.description: Planning a trip to Taiwan? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Taiwan?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B for most travellers to Taiwan. Japanese encephalitis may come up for rural travel or longer stays.

mainEntity.name: Do I need any travel vaccines for Taiwan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most travellers should have Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B up to date, plus routine vaccines such as measles. Taiwan has good medical infrastructure overall.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Taiwan should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

saudi-arabia.title: Vaccines for Saudi Arabia — Required & Recommended
saudi-arabia.description: Planning a trip to Saudi Arabia? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Saudi Arabia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Saudi Arabia. Rabies may come up for specific activities.

mainEntity.name: Is meningococcal vaccine required for Hajj?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Saudi Arabia requires quadrivalent meningococcal (ACWY) vaccination for all Hajj and Umrah travellers, given within 3 to 5 years and at least 10 days before arrival.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Saudi Arabia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure — earlier for Hajj or Umrah travellers because of the 10-day meningococcal rule.

jordan.title: Vaccines for Jordan — Required & Recommended
jordan.description: Planning a trip to Jordan? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Jordan?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Jordan. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Do I need any required vaccines to enter Jordan?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are no specific vaccines required for entry to Jordan from most countries. Yellow fever is not required for direct travel from countries without YF risk.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Jordan should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

israel.title: Vaccines for Israel — Required & Recommended
israel.description: Planning a trip to Israel? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Israel?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A for most travellers to Israel. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B, a polio booster, rabies, or typhoid — typhoid is suggested for travel to the West Bank and Gaza.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a polio booster for Israel?

acceptedAnswer.text: Poliovirus has been detected in environmental samples in Israel. CDC suggests adults previously vaccinated consider a one-time polio booster before travel.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Israel should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

oman.title: Vaccines for Oman — Required & Recommended
oman.description: Planning a trip to Oman? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Oman?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Oman. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is malaria a risk in Oman?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oman has no current malaria risk. Standard mosquito-bite prevention still helps against dengue and other insect-borne illness.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Oman should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Qatar?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Qatar should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

united-states.title: Vaccines for United States — Required & Recommended
united-states.description: Planning a trip to United States? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for the United States?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC's destination page focuses on routine vaccines. Make sure measles, flu, COVID-19, and Tdap are up to date. There are no specific travel vaccines recommended for most visitors.

mainEntity.name: Do I need any travel vaccines for the United States?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most travellers do not need travel-specific vaccines for the US. Routine vaccines, especially measles, should be up to date.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to the United States should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure if you'd like a routine review. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel.

canada.title: Vaccines for Canada — Required & Recommended
canada.description: Planning a trip to Canada? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC suggests Hepatitis A for most travellers to Canada, especially those visiting smaller cities or rural areas. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Do I need any travel vaccines for Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most travellers should have routine vaccines up to date. Hepatitis A is suggested for higher-risk activities like rural or village travel.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Canada should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

argentina.title: Vaccines for Argentina — Required & Recommended
argentina.description: Planning a trip to Argentina? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Argentina?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Argentina. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B, rabies, or yellow fever depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Do I need yellow fever vaccine for Iguazú Falls?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, CDC recommends yellow fever vaccine for travellers aged 9 months and older going to Corrientes and Misiones provinces, which includes Iguazú Falls.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Argentina should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

chile.title: Vaccines for Chile — Required & Recommended
chile.description: Planning a trip to Chile? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Chile?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Chile. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Chile?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is not required for entry to Chile.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Chile should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

colombia.title: Vaccines for Colombia — Required & Recommended
colombia.description: Planning a trip to Colombia? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Colombia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Colombia. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and chikungunya depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Colombia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Colombia requires yellow fever vaccination for all arriving travellers aged 1 year and older. CDC also recommends it for most travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Colombia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

dominican-republic.title: Vaccines for Dominican Republic — Required
dominican-republic.description: Planning a trip to the Dominican Republic? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Dominican Republic?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to the Dominican Republic. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B and rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Dominican Republic?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from specific Brazilian states with yellow fever transmission risk.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Dominican Republic should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

jamaica.title: Vaccines for Jamaica — Required & Recommended
jamaica.description: Planning a trip to Jamaica? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Jamaica?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A for most travellers to Jamaica. Your doctor may also discuss Hepatitis B, typhoid, or rabies depending on your itinerary.

mainEntity.name: Is malaria a risk in Jamaica?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Jamaica has no malaria. Dengue and Zika circulate, and leptospirosis risk rises after hurricane flooding.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Jamaica should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

ecuador.title: Vaccines for Ecuador — Required & Recommended
ecuador.description: Planning a trip to Ecuador? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Ecuador?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Ecuador. Your doctor may also discuss yellow fever, Hepatitis B, or rabies — yellow fever is recommended for Amazon-region travel.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Ecuador?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required for travellers arriving from countries with yellow fever transmission risk. CDC also recommends it for travellers visiting Amazon provinces below 2,300 m.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Ecuador should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

nigeria.title: Vaccines for Nigeria — Required & Recommended
nigeria.description: Planning a trip to Nigeria? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Nigeria?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Nigeria, plus prescription antimalarial medication for everyone. Chikungunya and cholera may also come up.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Nigeria?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you're arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Nigeria should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

ethiopia.title: Vaccines for Ethiopia — Required & Recommended
ethiopia.description: Planning a trip to Ethiopia? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Ethiopia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, typhoid, yellow fever, and meningococcal vaccines for most travellers to Ethiopia, plus prescription antimalarial medication for areas below 2,500 m.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Ethiopia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever is required for travellers arriving from countries with YF transmission risk. CDC also recommends it for most travellers, except trips limited to Afar or Somali regions.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Ethiopia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

ghana.title: Vaccines for Ghana — Required & Recommended
ghana.description: Planning a trip to Ghana? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Ghana?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Ghana, plus prescription antimalarial medication for everyone. Cholera, rabies, meningococcal, and mpox may also come up.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Ghana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Ghana requires yellow fever vaccination for all arriving travellers aged 9 months and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Ghana should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

uganda.title: Vaccines for Uganda — Required & Recommended
uganda.description: Planning a trip to Uganda? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Uganda?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Uganda, plus prescription antimalarial medication for everyone. Hepatitis B, meningococcal, cholera, and rabies may also come up.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Uganda?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Uganda requires yellow fever vaccination for all arriving travellers aged 1 year and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Uganda should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Yellow fever vaccine needs at least 10 days to take effect.

rwanda.title: Vaccines for Rwanda — Required & Recommended
rwanda.description: Planning a trip to Rwanda? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Rwanda?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends typhoid for most travellers to Rwanda, plus prescription antimalarial medication for the entire country. Cholera and rabies may also come up.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Rwanda?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is required if you're arriving from a country with YF transmission risk. Proof is required for travellers aged 1 year and older.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Rwanda should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

zambia.title: Vaccines for Zambia — Required & Recommended
zambia.description: Planning a trip to Zambia? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Zambia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A and typhoid for most travellers to Zambia, plus prescription antimalarial medication for the entire country. Hepatitis B, rabies, cholera, and mpox may also come up.

mainEntity.name: Is yellow fever required for Zambia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yellow fever vaccination is only required if you are arriving from a country with yellow fever transmission risk.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Zambia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

madagascar.title: Vaccines for Madagascar — Required & Recommended
madagascar.description: Planning a trip to Madagascar? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Madagascar?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC recommends Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and typhoid for most travellers to Madagascar. Your doctor may also discuss rabies, mpox, or chikungunya, plus antimalarial medication for travel outside Antananarivo.

mainEntity.name: Is malaria a risk in Madagascar?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Malaria is a risk in all areas except Antananarivo. CDC recommends prescription antimalarial medication for travellers heading outside the capital.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Madagascar should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

australia.title: Vaccines for Australia — Required & Recommended
australia.description: Planning a trip to Australia? See which vaccines CDC recommends for most travellers, what's required for entry, and when to book your travel clinic.

mainEntity.name: What vaccines do I need for Australia?

acceptedAnswer.text: CDC focuses on routine vaccines for Australia — make sure measles, flu, and Tdap are up to date. Hepatitis B and Japanese encephalitis may come up for longer stays or rural travel.

mainEntity.name: Do I need any travel vaccines for Australia?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most travellers do not need travel-specific vaccines for Australia. Routine vaccines, especially measles, should be up to date.

mainEntity.name: How long before travel to Australia should I get vaccines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Book a travel health appointment 4–6 weeks before departure. Some vaccines need multiple doses or a minimum interval before travel, so earlier is better.

hepatitis-a.title: Hepatitis A Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
hepatitis-a.description: Hepatitis A is a liver infection spread through contaminated food and water. CDC recommends the vaccine for most travellers to lower-income regions.

hepatitis-b.title: Hepatitis B Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
hepatitis-b.description: Hepatitis B is spread through blood, sex, and contaminated medical or cosmetic equipment. CDC recommends the vaccine for most travellers on longer trips.

typhoid.title: Typhoid Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
typhoid.description: Typhoid is a bacterial infection from contaminated food and water. CDC recommends the vaccine for travellers to most low- and middle-income countries.

japanese-encephalitis.title: Japanese Encephalitis Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
japanese-encephalitis.description: Japanese encephalitis is a mosquito-borne brain infection in rural Asia. CDC advises the vaccine for longer rural stays during rice-paddy season.

yellow-fever.title: Yellow Fever Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
yellow-fever.description: Yellow fever is a mosquito-borne virus in parts of Africa and South America. Proof of vaccination is required to enter many countries from a risk area.

rabies.title: Rabies Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
rabies.description: Rabies is a fatal viral disease from animal bites, mainly dogs, bats, and monkeys. Pre-travel vaccination simplifies treatment if you're bitten abroad.

cholera.title: Cholera Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
cholera.description: Cholera is a bacterial gut infection spread through contaminated water. CDC advises the vaccine for aid workers or travel to areas with an active outbreak.

chikungunya.title: Chikungunya Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
chikungunya.description: Chikungunya is a mosquito-borne virus causing fever and severe joint pain. CDC advises the vaccine during outbreaks or extended stays in risk areas.

meningococcal.title: Meningococcal Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
meningococcal.description: Meningococcal disease spreads through close contact and can cause meningitis. Required for Hajj or Umrah and advised for the African meningitis belt.

mpox.title: Mpox Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
mpox.description: Mpox is a viral illness spread through close skin-to-skin contact. CDC advises the vaccine for travellers anticipating new sexual partners in risk areas.

tick-borne-encephalitis.title: Tick-borne Encephalitis Vaccine for Travel
tick-borne-encephalitis.description: Tick-borne encephalitis is a brain infection from tick bites in parts of Europe and northern Asia. Worth considering for hikers and forest travellers.

mmr.title: MMR Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
mmr.description: MMR protects against measles, mumps, and rubella. CDC advises every international traveller to be fully vaccinated against measles before any trip.

dtap-tdap.title: Tdap Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
dtap-tdap.description: Tdap protects against tetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis. Diphtheria is now mainly seen in travellers, so a current booster is relevant before a trip.

polio.title: Polio Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
polio.description: Polio is a virus that can cause paralysis, spread through contaminated food and water. Adults travelling to at-risk countries may need an IPV booster.

influenza.title: Flu (Influenza) Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
influenza.description: Flu circulates year-round in the tropics and in opposite seasons in the southern hemisphere. A recent shot is advised for most international travellers.

chickenpox.title: Chickenpox (Varicella) Vaccine for Travel
chickenpox.description: Chickenpox is a highly contagious viral illness. CDC lists international travellers as a priority group for vaccination if not already immune.

shingles.title: Shingles Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
shingles.description: Shingles is a painful rash from reactivation of the chickenpox virus. Recommended for adults 50 and over as routine care, not specifically for travel.

covid-19.title: COVID-19 Vaccine for Travel — Who Needs It
covid-19.description: COVID-19 is a respiratory illness from SARS-CoV-2. CDC advises all eligible travellers to be up to date on COVID-19 vaccination before any trip.
